mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment growth above 200k per month signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer price inflation (CPI) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weak demand conditions that reduce corporate pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.15) with combined CPIAUCSL/HOUST declines in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity that drives equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO expansion phases fall below 0.5% average in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative yield curve (FEDFUNDS - 10Y Treasury) above 150bp signals impending recession risks that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in steep yield curve environments show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.1) with curve steepness.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates deteriorating economic fundamentals that trigger risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.2) with combined RSAFS/GDPC1 declines in 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high UNRATE environments exceed 1.0% average in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising capacity utilization signals overheating supply chains that prompt central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 2% in 3 months AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show positive correlation (Rank IC > 0.15) with INDPRO/FEDFUNDS co-movements in 3-year rolling windows.
